title: The Memory of the Stump Circle

features.name: stump circle
features.desc-on-touch: Twelve stumps arranged as they fell. Each ring you count is a year the children watched grow. Now the rings count backward toward what was taken.

features.name: shoot emergence
features.desc-on-touch: Bright green breaks from the heartwood. The stumps are teaching the forest how to regrow by showing it what was learned the first time.

features.name: teaching marks
features.desc-on-touch: The children carved their lessons into the wood. Now the forest reads them aloud. The carvings have become forest-language.

features.name: time-counting chamber
features.desc-on-touch: An archive marker where you can read how many seasons have passed since the circle learned it was a circle. The count never stops growing.

inscription: the children showed the stumps that growing backward was still growing; now the circle remembers teaching more than it remembers being trees

file.name: ChildrensLessons_Carved_RingCount_Comparisons.txt
file.note: Notches and words carved into living stumps, slowly being swallowed by regrowth. The forest learning to read the teaching before the teaching disappears.

reverie.title: REVERIE — stump circle at the hour of learning
narr: You both stand inside the circle while children move
between the stumps like the forest is teaching them
something with its absence. She has a knife. You watch
her mark the tallest stump with a date that will never
make sense to anyone who reads it later. The children
will understand. They learned their forest differently now.

exits.label: back toward the clearcut and the children who taught it
upward where the first ring still grows in memory
